---
title: "Multiple linear regression model"
output:
  word_document: default
  html_document: default
date: "2026-03-23"
---

```{r}
load(r"(D:\R Practice\#Classworks\#Example10\profits.Rdata)")
```

y: profit margins
x1: revenues
x2: share of investment

```{r}
pairs(profits,
      panel = panel.smooth,
      main = "scatterplot matrix")
```
We estimate the model using the lm function

```{r}
lm1 <- lm(y~ x1+x2, data = profits)
# Add ( ) around your formula and data
ml2 <- lm(y ~ ., data = profits)
```

# This tells R to actually open the package you just installed
library(scatterplot3d)
# Now your 3D plot will work!

We make a short description of the results through the summary function

```{r}
ex1 <- scatterplot3d(profits[,2],
                     profits[,3],
                     profits[,1],
                     pch = 20)
ex1$plane3d(lm1, col = "red")
```
